Notice Of Motion To Deny Class Certification And Strike Paga Allegations
Off Calendar Oct-21-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. MELISSA GRANT, ESQ. of Capstone Law APC, Tel No. 310-556-4811 appearing for the Plaintiffs;
Attys. HAROLD M. BRODY, ESQ. (Tel No. 310-284-5625) and ROBERT A. ESCALANTE, ESQ. (Tel No. 310-284-5623) of Proskauer Rose LLP appearing for Defendant Swissport USA, Inc.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANT SWISSPORT USA, INC.'S Motion To Deny Class Certification And Strike Paga Allegations, the motion to deny class certification in its entirety and to strike the PAGA claim is denied. Although Plaintiffs waited three years to seek class certification, the trial court has the discretion to deny or grant certification based on a plaintiff's dilatoriness in filing for certification. (Massey v. Bank of America (1976) 56 Cal.App.3d 29, 32.) Here, certification of the Class, the Meal Premium Subclass, and the Regular Rate Subclass is warranted because common questions of fact and law predominate. Furthermore, as to the Class, Meal Premium Subclass, and Regular Rate Subclass, Plaintiffs have demonstrated that the claims are typical of putative class members' claims, Plaintiffs and counsel will fairly and adequately represent the interests of the proposed class and subclasses, the proposed class and subclasses are ascertainable and sufficiently numerous, and class treatment is superior to individual suits. (Sav-on Drug Stores, Inc. v. Superior Court (2004) 34 Cal.4th 319, 326; CCP section 382.) Defendant's argument that the Airline Deregulation Act of 1978 pre-empts California's meal and rest period requirements is unavailing. (See Rowe v, N.H. Motor Transp. Ass'n. (2008) 552 U.S. 364, 368, 370; People ex rel. Harris v. Pac Anchor Trans Inc. (2014) 59 Cal.4th 772, 779-86.) Furthermore, Defendant's motion to strike the PAGA claim because it is unmanageable is unfounded. First, the PAGA claim need not meet the requirements for class certification. (Arias v. Superior Court (2009) 46 Cal.4th 969, 980-84.) Secondly, Defendant's system wide Meal Rules policy and its policy regarding the payment of overtime and meal breaks raise predominate, common issues of fact and law which indicate that the PAGA claim is in fact manageable. Plaintiffs counsel shall prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

APPEARANCES: Atty. MELISSA GRANT of Capstone Law, A.P.C., Tel No. (310) 556-4811 appearing telephonically for the Plaintiffs;
Atty. KENNETH D. SULZER of Proskauer Rose, LLP, Tel No. (310) 284-5663 appearing telephonically for the Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFFS JOSHUA WILLIAMS AND ROGER VALLE'S Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint is granted. Plaintiffs may file the proposed first amended complaint within 10 days of entry of this order. "There is a policy of great liberality in permitting amendments to the pleadings at any stage of the proceeding." (Berman v. Bromberg (1997) 56 Cal.App.4th 936, 945.) However, the trial court has discretion to deny a motion for leave to amend if the plaintiff was dilatory and the defendant will be prejudiced by the amendment. (Hirsa v. Sup. Ct. (1981) 118 Cal.App.3d 486, 490.) While Defendant has established that Plaintiffs were dilatory in seeking amendment as to the Labor Code wage and hour claims, Defendant has not established that they will be unduly prejudiced by the amendment. The trial date is not until October 2015, giving Defendant time to conduct additional discovery, if necessary. Further, wage and hour violations were pled in the original complaint in the PAGA and Violation of Bus. and Prof. Code sec. 17200 causes of action. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: A. James Robertson, II; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/AJR)
